title: Flaky Light Jamaican Fish Hash
description: A light and flaky Jamaican dish featuring shredded fish, with a hint of mustard and a springy texture, cooked to perfection in a wok. This pescatarian-friendly recipe is perfect for a quick and easy meal.
instructions: Heat the wok over medium heat and add the melted butter to coat the bottom.
Add the shredded fish to the wok and cook until it is lightly browned, breaking it up into small pieces as it cooks.
In a small bowl, mix together the flour, mustard powder, paprika, salt, and pepper, and add this mixture to the wok, stirring to combine.
Add the chopped scallions to the wok and stir to combine.
In a small bowl, whisk together the beaten egg and water, and add this mixture to the wok, stirring to combine.
Continue cooking the mixture in the wok, stirring constantly, until it is lightly browned and crispy, using a spatula to break it up into small pieces as it cooks.
Use the spatula to shape the mixture into a flat, round shape, and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, until the bottom is lightly browned and crispy.
Loosen the hash with the spatula and flip it over, cooking for an additional 2-3 minutes, until the other side is also lightly browned and crispy.
Remove the hash from the wok and serve hot.
